title: Proof Construction and Theorems in Logic
description: A flowchart illustrating the process of proving theorems using logical definitions, rules of inference, and established proof techniques.
relationships: Definitions are used to establish foundational concepts
Axioms are accepted as true starting points
Rules of Inference logically connect statements
Propositions are proven using definitions and rules
Lemmas are intermediate results used to prove Theorems
Theorems are significant statements proven from axioms and definitions
Proof Techniques provide the structural method for establishing truth
